_comment: DatumV model catalog — v3 manifest read by the in-app Model Manager.
Schema shape:
- Each top-level `entries` element is an entry family (e.g. 'YOLOX').
Entry-level fields (name, summary, description, tasks, licenseIds,
attributions, cardFile, heroImageFile) describe the family as a whole.
- A `variants[]` array carries the install-handle-bearing cuts
(e.g. yolox-s, yolox-nano; florence-2-base-ft-fp16,
florence-2-base-ft-quantized). Variants own hardware,
approxSizeMb, versions[], placeholder / requiresHfLogin flags,
Kind / Python config — anything install / dispatch-related.
- The Model Manager UI shows one list row per entry; the detail
pane renders entry chrome (hero + card markdown) plus a variant
tab strip when more than one variant is present.
Install handle: `variant.id`. The downloader, residency manager, and
every install/uninstall/probe surface key on this string. Variant ids
are validated globally unique across the manifest so a single index
lookup resolves to (entry, variant) at runtime.
License metadata + text live centrally in `<repo>/licenses/index.json`.
Per-entry `licenseIds` references entries there; this manifest no longer
carries its own licenses block.

entries.name: MiniLM-L6 Embeddings
entries.summary: Turn sentences into vectors so you can find similar ones with cosine similarity. The fastest, smallest English embedder — start here for semantic search and clustering.
entries.description: Tiny, fast sentence embeddings (384-dim). Good default for similarity search.
attributions: sentence-transformers — Reimers & Gurevych (UKP Lab, TU Darmstadt)
HuggingFace optimum (ONNX export)

variants.summary: Turn sentences into vectors so you can find similar ones with cosine similarity. The fastest, smallest English embedder — start here for semantic search and clustering.

entries.name: BGE Small (English)
entries.summary: MiniLM-size English embedder with materially better retrieval accuracy. Drop-in replacement for MiniLM when you can spare ~50% more disk.
entries.description: MTEB-leaderboard-grade English embeddings at MiniLM size.
attributions: BAAI — Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence

variants.summary: MiniLM-size English embedder with materially better retrieval accuracy. Drop-in replacement for MiniLM when you can spare ~50% more disk.

entries.name: Jina Embeddings v2 (Long Context)
entries.summary: English embedder that handles 8,000-token chunks (≈10 typical web pages at once). Use when you want to retrieve whole documents instead of slicing them into paragraphs first.
entries.description: 8K-context English embeddings, 768-dim. Good for whole-document retrieval.
attributions: Jina AI

variants.summary: English embedder that handles 8,000-token chunks (≈10 typical web pages at once). Use when you want to retrieve whole documents instead of slicing them into paragraphs first.

entries.name: BGE Reranker (Base)
entries.summary: Re-orders a list of candidate passages by how well they answer a query. Use as the second pass after a vector-similarity search to put the best match at the top.
entries.description: Cross-encoder reranker — much more accurate than cosine similarity for top-k retrieval.
attributions: BAAI — Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence
Joshua Lochner / Xenova (ONNX export)

variants.summary: Re-orders a list of candidate passages by how well they answer a query. Use as the second pass after a vector-similarity search to put the best match at the top.

entries.name: Twitter RoBERTa Sentiment
entries.summary: Tells whether a sentence sounds positive, negative, or neutral. Trained on tweets but works on any short English text — reviews, comments, support tickets.
entries.description: 3-class sentiment (positive / negative / neutral). Generalizes beyond Twitter.
attributions: Cardiff NLP — Cardiff University
Joshua Lochner / Xenova (ONNX export)

variants.summary: Tells whether a sentence sounds positive, negative, or neutral. Trained on tweets but works on any short English text — reviews, comments, support tickets.

entries.name: Toxic-BERT
entries.summary: Scores English text on six kinds of toxicity (toxic, severe, obscene, threat, insult, identity-hate). Use for moderating user-generated content.
entries.description: 6-label toxicity (toxic / severe / obscene / threat / insult / identity-hate).
attributions: Unitary
Joshua Lochner / Xenova (ONNX export)

variants.summary: Scores English text on six kinds of toxicity (toxic, severe, obscene, threat, insult, identity-hate). Use for moderating user-generated content.

entries.name: Florence-2 base-ft
entries.summary: One image model that does many things: write a caption, find objects, read text, or answer questions about the picture. Pick what it does per call via a task prompt.
entries.description: Microsoft's unified vision-language model. All variants expose the same four task prompts (caption, detailed caption, more-detailed caption, OCR-with-region) — they differ only in weight precision and target hardware.
attributions: Microsoft Research

variants.summary: One image model that does many things: write a caption, find objects, read text, or answer questions about the picture. Pick what it does per call via a task prompt. Half-precision GPU build.
Same all-in-one image model as Florence-2 fp16 — captioning, OCR, detection, VQA from task prompts — half the disk, runs comfortably on CPU.

entries.name: ViT-GPT2 Image Captioner
entries.summary: Describe an image in one sentence. Classic baseline captioner — fast and adequate; reach for Florence-2 if you also need OCR or object detection from the same model.
entries.description: Classic ViT encoder + GPT-2 decoder. Vanilla 'describe this image' baseline.
attributions: nlpconnect — Ankur Singh et al.

variants.summary: Describe an image in one sentence. Classic baseline captioner — fast and adequate; reach for Florence-2 if you also need OCR or object detection from the same model.

entries.name: YOLOX
entries.summary: Find and box common objects in a photo (people, cars, dogs — 80 COCO categories). Apache-2.0 alternative to YOLOv8. Pick a size variant: nano/tiny for edge, s/m for CPU defaults, l/x for GPU + accuracy, darknet for the legacy backbone.
entries.description: All variants share the same 80-class COCO label space, the same preprocessing (BGR, 0–255, letterbox to a multiple of 32), and the same decode logic — they differ only in backbone size. Swap variant ids in a query and the only thing that changes is mAP and latency.
attributions: Megvii Inc. — YOLOX (Ge, Liu, Wang, Li, Sun)

variants.summary: Smallest YOLOX — fits on phones and edge devices (~4 MB). Use when latency and disk matter more than peak accuracy.
Step up from YOLOX-Nano with meaningfully better accuracy at minimal extra cost. The pragmatic pick for edge devices that can spare a few MB.
Find and box common objects in a photo (people, cars, dogs — 80 COCO categories). Recommended starting point for object detection.
Mid-size YOLOX. Runs comfortably on CPU; flies on GPU. The default 'I want quality without a big model' pick.
Larger YOLOX — sharper accuracy than M on small / crowded objects. Plan for GPU.
Biggest YOLOX — highest accuracy in the family. Pick when GPU is available and you want the fewest false positives.
Original YOLOX backbone — included for reproducibility. The CSPDarknet variants (M / L) generally beat it on accuracy-per-cost; pick this only when you need this specific architecture for comparison.

entries.name: RT-DETR-R18
entries.summary: Real-time object detector with a ResNet-18 backbone. COCO 80-class output. Three precision tiers so callers can pick footprint vs hardware.
entries.description: All variants share the architecture, label vocabulary, and decode path — they differ only in weight precision (fp32/fp16/INT8). fp16 is the GPU sweet spot; INT8 fits CPU/NPU; fp32 is the reference build.
attributions: Baidu Inc. — RT-DETR (Lv, Xu, Zhao et al., PaddlePaddle team)
PekingU (HuggingFace mirror)
onnx-community (ONNX export)

variants.summary: Modern transformer-based object detector — same 80 COCO categories as YOLOX but skips the non-maximum-suppression post-processing step. Cleaner output pipeline, slightly different speed profile.
Half-precision RT-DETR-R18 — same detector, half the disk. Pick on GPUs with native fp16 for a small speedup at no quality cost.
Aggressively quantized RT-DETR-R18 — ~quarter the disk, CPU-friendly. Modest accuracy drop on small / crowded scenes; best when deployment size matters.

entries.name: MobileSAM (Segment Anything)
entries.summary: Click or draw a box on something in an image and get a clean cutout mask of it. The phone-sized cousin of Meta's Segment Anything — works from point or box prompts.
entries.description: MobileSAM ViT-T encoder + SAM mask decoders (single + multi-mask). Interactive segmentation from clicks, boxes, or mask hints. ~60 MB total.
attributions: Meta AI Research — Segment Anything (Kirillov, Mintun, Ravi et al.)
Chaoning Zhang et al. — MobileSAM (Kyung Hee University)

variants.summary: Click or draw a box on something in an image and get a clean cutout mask of it. The phone-sized cousin of Meta's Segment Anything — works from point or box prompts.

entries.name: U²-Net Lite / u2netp (Salient Object Segmentation)
entries.summary: Cut the main subject of a photo out of its background. Lightweight default (~5 MB) — use for product shots, profile pictures, anywhere you want a transparent background.
entries.description: Distilled U²-Net for binary salient-object (foreground) segmentation. 4.7M params, ~4.7 MB, 320×320 input. Output is a single-channel mask separating subject from background — pair with image_cutout() for background removal. Recommended default; full u2net is only worth the disk hit on tricky fine-edge cases (hair, fur).
attributions: Xuebin Qin, Zichen Zhang, Chenyang Huang, Masood Dehghan, Osmar R. Zaiane, Martin Jagersand — U²-Net (University of Alberta)

variants.summary: Cut the main subject of a photo out of its background. Lightweight default (~5 MB) — use for product shots, profile pictures, anywhere you want a transparent background.

entries.name: U²-Net Full (Salient Object Segmentation)
entries.summary: Full-size salient-object cutout. Same job as the Lite variant but with cleaner edges around hair, fur, lace, and other thin structures.
entries.description: Full U²-Net (176M params, ~170 MB, 320×320 input). Same task as u2netp but with sharper edges on hair / fur / lace / thin structures. Pick this over u2netp only when fine-detail boundaries matter and disk + latency aren't constrained.
attributions: Xuebin Qin, Zichen Zhang, Chenyang Huang, Masood Dehghan, Osmar R. Zaiane, Martin Jagersand — U²-Net (University of Alberta)

variants.summary: Full-size salient-object cutout. Same job as the Lite variant but with cleaner edges around hair, fur, lace, and other thin structures.

entries.name: DPT-Large (Monocular Depth)
entries.summary: Guess how far away each pixel of a photo is. High-quality relative depth (no absolute scale) — pick on GPU when accuracy on fine details matters.
entries.description: Intel ISL Dense Prediction Transformer for monocular depth estimation. 384×384 input. Outputs relative depth (not metric).
attributions: Intel ISL — Ranftl, Bochkovskiy, Koltun (DPT / MiDaS)

variants.summary: Guess how far away each pixel of a photo is. High-quality relative depth (no absolute scale) — pick on GPU when accuracy on fine details matters.

entries.name: MiDaS v2.1 Small (Monocular Depth)
entries.summary: Lightweight relative depth from a single image — ~50 ms per image on consumer CPU. Use when latency and disk matter more than absolute quality.
entries.description: Lightweight EfficientNet-Lite3 + depth-decoder monocular depth estimator. ~21M params, 256×256 BGR input. ~50ms per image on consumer CPU. Sibling to DPT-Large but ~16× smaller — pick this when latency / disk matter more than absolute quality.
attributions: Intel ISL — Ranftl, Lasinger, Hafner, Schindler, Koltun (MiDaS)

variants.summary: Lightweight relative depth from a single image — ~50 ms per image on consumer CPU. Use when latency and disk matter more than absolute quality.

entries.name: Depth Anything V2 Small (Monocular Depth)
entries.summary: Sharper relative-depth estimator with a stronger backbone than DPT / MiDaS. Recommended modern default for monocular depth — best quality-per-MB in this catalog.
entries.description: Current SOTA permissive-license general-purpose monocular depth. DINOv2 ViT-S encoder + DPT-style decoder, ~24M params. Faster than DPT-Large, often sharper boundaries. Relative depth (not metric). Recommended modern default — best quality-per-MB in this catalog.
attributions: Lihe Yang, Bingyi Kang, Zilong Huang, Zhen Zhao, Xiaogang Xu, Jiashi Feng, Hengshuang Zhao — Depth Anything V2 (TikTok / HKU)
onnx-community (ONNX export)

variants.summary: Sharper relative-depth estimator with a stronger backbone than DPT / MiDaS. Recommended modern default for monocular depth — best quality-per-MB in this catalog.

entries.name: Depth Anything V2 Base (Monocular Depth)
entries.summary: Mid-size Depth Anything V2 — better detail on fine boundaries than the Small variant. Pick on CPU when accuracy beats throughput.
entries.description: Mid-size variant of Depth Anything V2 — DINOv2 ViT-B encoder, ~97M params. Quality close to the V2-Large variant without V2-Large's CC-BY-NC-4.0 license issue (V2-Large is non-commercial; V2-Base is Apache-2.0). Pick over the Small variant when accuracy on fine detail matters more than CPU throughput.
attributions: Lihe Yang, Bingyi Kang, Zilong Huang, Zhen Zhao, Xiaogang Xu, Jiashi Feng, Hengshuang Zhao — Depth Anything V2 (TikTok / HKU)
onnx-community (ONNX export)

variants.summary: Mid-size Depth Anything V2 — better detail on fine boundaries than the Small variant. Pick on CPU when accuracy beats throughput.

entries.name: Depth Anything V3 Large (Depth + Pose)
entries.summary: Latest generation Depth Anything — best monocular relative depth quality in this catalog. Bonus: also predicts camera pose alongside depth, closer to a unified geometric-understanding model than a depth-only network.
entries.description: Latest Depth Anything generation. Successor to V2 from a partially-new team — large DINOv2 backbone, ~1.4 GB total (external-data ONNX). Headline change: **Apache-2.0 across all variants including Large** (V2-Large was CC-BY-NC, kept it out of the catalog). Additionally produces **camera pose estimation** as a secondary output, not just depth — closer to a unified geometric-understanding model than a depth-only network. Should give visibly smoother surfaces than V2-Base on the surface-noise issues that show up in the depth+Poisson pipeline.
attributions: Haotong Lin, Sili Chen, Jun Hao Liew, Donny Y. Chen, Zhenyu Li, Guang Shi, Jiashi Feng, Bingyi Kang — Depth Anything V3
onnx-community / Xenova (ONNX export)

variants.summary: Latest generation Depth Anything — best monocular relative depth quality in this catalog. Bonus: also predicts camera pose alongside depth, closer to a unified geometric-understanding model than a depth-only network.

entries.name: Metric3D V2 (Metric Depth + Normals)
entries.summary: Estimate per-pixel metric depth (real-world meters) + surface normals from a single image. Three model sizes × fp32 and fp16 precision.
entries.description: All variants share the same metric depth + normals output. Size axis trades accuracy for speed; precision axis trades disk footprint for GPU native fp16 throughput.
attributions: Mu Hu, Wei Yin, Chi Zhang, Zhipeng Cai, Xiaoxiao Long, Hao Chen, Kaixuan Wang, Gang Yu, Chunhua Shen, Shaojie Shen — Metric3D V2 (HKU + Shanghai AI Lab)

variants.summary: Lightweight Metric3D V2 — ViT-Small backbone, CPU-friendly. Pick for edge / latency-bound use, or as a quick first-pass before a heavier model.

Lightweight Metric3D V2 — ViT-Small backbone, CPU-friendly. Pick for edge / latency-bound use, or as a quick first-pass before a heavier model. ~Half the disk and model-load memory, same outdoor-strong metric depth + surface normals outputs.

Outputs **real-world metric depth** and **surface normals** from a single image in one forward pass. Specialty: outdoor scenes (parking lots, landscapes, mixed distances) where indoor-trained metric depth models fall off.
Half-precision build of Metric3D V2 Large — ~half the disk and model-load memory, same outdoor-strong metric depth + surface normals outputs.
Largest Metric3D V2 variant — ViT-Giant backbone (~1B params, ~5.5 GB). Best quality on hard outdoor scenes; GPU required.
Half-precision build of Metric3D V2 Giant — ~half the disk and model-load memory, same outdoor-strong metric depth + surface normals outputs.

entries.name: ZoeDepth (NYU+KITTI, Metric Depth)
entries.summary: Metric depth in meters from a single image. DPT-Large backbone with NYU indoor + KITTI outdoor calibration. fp32 reference; fp16 for half-size deployment.
entries.description: Use when absolute scale matters (3D reconstruction, AR overlays). fp16 cuts disk to ~660 MB with no quality cost on GPU runtimes.
attributions: Shariq Farooq Bhat, Reiner Birkl, Diana Wofk, Peter Wonka, Matthias Müller — ZoeDepth (Intel ISL)
Heliosoph (Optimum / torch.onnx.export from Intel/zoedepth-nyu-kitti PyTorch weights)

variants.summary: Monocular depth in **real-world meters** (not just relative). Use when you need consistent scale across photos for 3D reconstruction, distance measurement, or AR overlays.
Same real-world-meter depth as ZoeDepth NYU+KITTI, half the disk. Pick for tighter deployment footprint with no quality change on most CPUs.

entries.name: TripoSR (Single-Image to 3D Mesh)
entries.summary: Turn a single photo of one object into a 3D mesh you can rotate, render, or 3D-print. The model hallucinates the back side the camera couldn't see. Object-scale (one subject centered in frame), not whole-scene.
entries.description: Single-image to 3D mesh in one shot. DINOv2 ViT image encoder + triplane decoder + implicit field, all trained jointly so the model can hallucinate the back / occluded sides of an object from a single front-facing photo — the complement to the depth+Poisson pipeline (which only captures what the camera sees). Object-scale, not scene-scale: works best on a single subject centered in frame. Outputs a watertight triangle mesh ready for browser rendering or 3D printing.
attributions: Stability AI + Tripo AI / VAST-AI-Research — TripoSR (Tochilkin, Pankratz, Liu, Huang, Letts, Li, Liang, Laforte, Jampani, Cao, 2024)
Heliosoph (ONNX export from upstream PyTorch weights)

variants.summary: Turn a single photo of one object into a 3D mesh you can rotate, render, or 3D-print. The model hallucinates the back side the camera couldn't see. Object-scale (one subject centered in frame), not whole-scene.

entries.name: GLPN-NYU (Lightweight Depth)
entries.summary: Lightweight monocular depth from a different model family than DPT / Depth Anything. Mostly interesting for side-by-side comparison demos; the modern Depth Anything V2 Small beats it on quality.
entries.description: Global-Local Path Network for monocular depth from KAIST (2022). EfficientFormer encoder + custom decoder, ~52M params. Older than Depth Anything V2 but solid as a lightweight baseline — useful as an architectural-diversity row in a comparison demo (different backbone family from the DPT / Depth Anything DINOv2 lineage).
attributions: Doyeon Kim, Woonghyun Ka, Pyungwhan Ahn, Donggyu Joo, Sehwan Chun, Junmo Kim — GLPN (KAIST)
Joshua Lochner / Xenova (ONNX export)

variants.summary: Lightweight monocular depth from a different model family than DPT / Depth Anything. Mostly interesting for side-by-side comparison demos; the modern Depth Anything V2 Small beats it on quality.

entries.name: MediaPipe Face Detector
entries.summary: Find face bounding boxes + 6 landmarks per face in a photo. fp32 reference build + INT8 for CPU/NPU.
entries.description: Google's BlazeFace short-range detector. Same model topology in both variants; INT8 halves disk + accelerates CPU.
attributions: Google MediaPipe team
Zak Murez (MediaPipePyTorch PyTorch port)
Qualcomm AI Hub (ONNX export)

variants.summary: Find faces in a photo, with eye / nose / mouth keypoints per face. Tiny (~3 MB), Apache-2.0 throughout. Use as the first stage of any face workflow.
Same face detector as MediaPipe Face, quantized to half the size. Slight accuracy drop on small / distant faces — use on CPU / NPU / mobile.

entries.name: PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4 Detection
entries.summary: Find text regions in an image (no recognition). Tiny (~5 MB). Pair with TrOCR or Florence-2 to read what's inside the boxes.
entries.description: Text-region detector for OCR pipelines. Tiny (~5 MB). Outputs polygon coordinates per text region. Pair with TrOCR or Florence-2 for end-to-end OCR.
attributions: PaddlePaddle Authors — Baidu Inc. (PaddleOCR contributors)

variants.summary: Find text regions in an image (no recognition). Tiny (~5 MB). Pair with TrOCR or Florence-2 to read what's inside the boxes.

entries.name: TrOCR Base Printed
entries.summary: Encoder-decoder model for reading printed text from cropped image regions. Pick fp32 for the reference build; fp16 for half-disk GPU deployments.
entries.description: Architecture, training data, and tokenizer are identical across variants; only precision changes.
attributions: Microsoft Research — TrOCR (Li, Lv, Yu, Cui, Lu, Wei, 2021)
Heliosoph (Optimum ONNX export from microsoft/trocr-base-printed PyTorch weights)

variants.summary: Read printed text from a single cropped line image. Pair with PaddleOCR detection (or Florence-2) for end-to-end document OCR.
Same printed-text recognizer as TrOCR Base Printed, half the disk. Pick when deployment footprint matters.

entries.name: Real-ESRGAN x4v3 (4x Upscaler)
entries.summary: Upscale a photo by 4× while cleaning up real-world artifacts (JPEG compression, sensor noise, mild blur). Tiny (~5 MB) but works well on real photos and screenshots.
entries.description: General-purpose 4x image super-resolution. Tiny (~5 MB) but punches above its weight on real photos and screenshots. Tile large inputs to manage memory.
attributions: © 2021 Xintao Wang — Real-ESRGAN (Tencent ARC Lab)

variants.summary: Upscale a photo by 4× while cleaning up real-world artifacts (JPEG compression, sensor noise, mild blur). Tiny (~5 MB) but works well on real photos and screenshots.

entries.name: SwinIR Real-World SR (4×)
entries.summary: Higher-quality 4× upscaler than Real-ESRGAN x4v3 on the model's pinned 64×64 tiles. Also restores real-world degradations during the upscale. Plan for GPU.
entries.description: Swin Transformer for Image Restoration — real-world super-resolution variant. Upscales by 4× while cleaning up real-world degradations (compression artifacts, sensor noise, mild blur). SwinIR-L config, 240-dim embeddings. Pinned 64×64 input → 256×256 output; tile larger inputs.
attributions: Jingyun Liang, Jiezhang Cao, Guolei Sun, Kai Zhang, Luc Van Gool, Radu Timofte — SwinIR (ETH Zurich + others)

variants.summary: Higher-quality 4× upscaler than Real-ESRGAN x4v3 on the model's pinned 64×64 tiles. Also restores real-world degradations during the upscale. Plan for GPU.

entries.name: SwinIR Color Denoising (σ=25)
entries.summary: Removes moderate-noise color noise (the σ=25 benchmark level). Pick when you have known synthetic-noise conditions; for real-world phone-camera noise prefer SCUNet Color Real PSNR.
entries.description: SwinIR color denoising variant trained for Gaussian noise σ=25. SwinIR-M config, 180-dim embeddings. Pinned 128×128 input. Use for the standard denoising-benchmark σ=25 reference; for blind real-world noise pick scunet-color-real instead.
attributions: Jingyun Liang, Jiezhang Cao, Guolei Sun, Kai Zhang, Luc Van Gool, Radu Timofte — SwinIR (ETH Zurich + others)

variants.summary: Removes moderate-noise color noise (the σ=25 benchmark level). Pick when you have known synthetic-noise conditions; for real-world phone-camera noise prefer SCUNet Color Real PSNR.

entries.name: SCUNet (Image Denoising)
entries.summary: Swin-Conv UNet for image denoising. Pick a noise level (15/25/50 sigma) × colour or gray; or one of two real-photo finetunes (PSNR vs GAN).
entries.description: All variants share the same architecture. Noise-level variants target synthetic Gaussian noise at the named sigma; the *-real-* variants are tuned on real photographs (PSNR for high-fidelity restoration, GAN for perceptual quality).
attributions: Kai Zhang, Yawei Li, Jingyun Liang, Jiezhang Cao, Yulun Zhang, Hao Tang, Deng-Ping Fan, Radu Timofte, Luc Van Gool — SCUNet

variants.summary: Real-world color photo denoiser. Handles unknown sensor + JPEG + compression artifacts without needing the noise level. Recommended default for general photo cleanup.
Same real-world color denoiser, sharper and more 'photographic' output. Invents plausible texture (hair, fabric). Pick for consumer cleanup; avoid when fidelity matters.
Color Gaussian-noise denoiser tuned for light noise (σ=15 — ISO-grain territory). Use for matched-condition denoising or benchmark reproduction.
Color Gaussian-noise denoiser tuned for moderate noise (σ=25 — typical night-photo level). The standard benchmark reference.
Color Gaussian-noise denoiser tuned for heavy noise (σ=50 — extreme low-light). Aggressive enough to flatten heavy noise; over-smooths clean inputs.
Single-channel grayscale denoiser at σ=15. ~3× faster than running color SCUNet on duplicated channels. For medical, document, and scientific imagery.
Single-channel grayscale denoiser at σ=25. Standard reference for grayscale denoising — chest X-rays, scanned documents, B&W photos.
Single-channel grayscale denoiser at σ=50. For heavily-noisy grayscale workflows — low-light astrophotography, degraded scans.

entries.name: MobileNetV2 (ImageNet Classification)
entries.summary: Label what's in a photo with one of 1,000 ImageNet categories (e.g. 'tabby cat', 'sports car'). Tiny CPU-friendly baseline.
entries.description: Tiny 1000-class ImageNet image classifier (~14 MB). CPU-friendly baseline for whole-image labelling, embedding extraction, or as a backbone for fine-tuning. Bundles the ImageNet-1K class label file.
attributions: Google AI Research — MobileNetV2 (Sandler, Howard, Zhu, Zhmoginov, Chen)
ONNX Model Zoo (ONNX export)

variants.summary: Label what's in a photo with one of 1,000 ImageNet categories (e.g. 'tabby cat', 'sports car'). Tiny CPU-friendly baseline.

entries.name: Kokoro v1.0 (Text-to-Speech)
entries.summary: Turn text into spoken audio in eleven bundled voices (American + British, male + female). Tiny (~320 MB) and surprisingly natural for its size.
entries.description: 82M-param TTS with 11 bundled voices (American + British, male + female). Punches above its weight.
attributions: hexgrad — Kokoro author

variants.summary: Turn text into spoken audio in eleven bundled voices (American + British, male + female). Tiny (~320 MB) and surprisingly natural for its size.

entries.name: Bark (Text to Speech)
entries.summary: Suno's generative TTS. Bark-small is the laptop-friendly cut; Bark is the full-quality model.
entries.description: Both variants share the Python worker and dispatch path; they differ only in model size.
attributions: Suno — Bark authors

variants.summary: Generate expressive speech — including laughs, sighs, and embedded sound effects — from text. Runs as a Python worker. Pick the small variant for affordable GPU inference.
Full Suno Bark TTS — more natural speech than Bark Small at 3–4× the inference cost. Use when expressiveness matters more than throughput.

entries.name: AbsoluteReality + Hyper-SD (4-step)
entries.summary: Generate photoreal-leaning images from text prompts in just 4 sampling steps. SD 1.5-based all-purpose workhorse, SFW.
entries.description: SFW general-purpose SD 1.5 fine-tune + 4-step distillation. Photorealistic-ish workhorse.
attributions: Lykon — AbsoluteReality (SD 1.5 fine-tune)
ByteDance — Hyper-SD LoRA (Ren, Xia, Zhang et al.)
CompVis / Stability AI — Stable Diffusion 1.5 base

variants.summary: Generate photoreal-leaning images from text prompts in just 4 sampling steps. SD 1.5-based all-purpose workhorse, SFW.

entries.name: DreamShaper + Hyper-SD (4-step)
entries.summary: Generate stylized fantasy, painterly, or illustrated images from text prompts in 4 steps. Best for D&D-style art and concept illustration.
entries.description: Stylized SFW SD 1.5 fine-tune — fantasy, painterly, illustrative. Good for D&D-style art.
attributions: Lykon — DreamShaper (SD 1.5 fine-tune)
ByteDance — Hyper-SD LoRA (Ren, Xia, Zhang et al.)
CompVis / Stability AI — Stable Diffusion 1.5 base

variants.summary: Generate stylized fantasy, painterly, or illustrated images from text prompts in 4 steps. Best for D&D-style art and concept illustration.

entries.name: epiCRealism + Hyper-SD (4-step)
entries.summary: Generate photoreal environments — landscapes, architecture, interiors — in 4 steps. Pick when the subject is 'a place' rather than 'a person'.
entries.description: Photorealistic environments + natural lighting. Pick over Realistic Vision when the subject is 'a place'.
attributions: emilianJR — epiCRealism (SD 1.5 fine-tune)
ByteDance — Hyper-SD LoRA (Ren, Xia, Zhang et al.)
CompVis / Stability AI — Stable Diffusion 1.5 base

variants.summary: Generate photoreal environments — landscapes, architecture, interiors — in 4 steps. Pick when the subject is 'a place' rather than 'a person'.

entries.name: Mo-Di + Hyper-SD (4-step)
entries.summary: Generate images in modern Disney / Pixar style — the model expects the activator phrase `modern disney style` somewhere in your prompt. 4 sampling steps.
entries.description: Modern Disney/Pixar style fine-tune. Activator: 'modern disney style'.
attributions: Nitrosocke — Mo-Di Diffusion (SD 1.5 fine-tune)
ByteDance — Hyper-SD LoRA (Ren, Xia, Zhang et al.)
CompVis / Stability AI — Stable Diffusion 1.5 base

variants.summary: Generate images in modern Disney / Pixar style — the model expects the activator phrase `modern disney style` somewhere in your prompt. 4 sampling steps.

entries.name: Openjourney + Hyper-SD (4-step)
entries.summary: Generate images in a Midjourney-v4 look — activator phrase is `mdjrny-v4 style`. 4 sampling steps.
entries.description: Midjourney-v4-style fine-tune. Activator: 'mdjrny-v4 style'.
attributions: PromptHero — Openjourney (SD 1.5 fine-tune)
ByteDance — Hyper-SD LoRA (Ren, Xia, Zhang et al.)
CompVis / Stability AI — Stable Diffusion 1.5 base

variants.summary: Generate images in a Midjourney-v4 look — activator phrase is `mdjrny-v4 style`. 4 sampling steps.

entries.name: Realistic Vision V6 + Hyper-SD (4-step)
entries.summary: Generate photoreal portraits — sharper faces and skin texture than the other Hyper variants. More NSFW-permissive base.
entries.description: Photorealistic portraits. More NSFW-permissive than the other Hyper variants.
attributions: SG161222 — Realistic Vision V6 (SD 1.5 fine-tune)
Stability AI — sd-vae-ft-mse (paired VAE)
ByteDance — Hyper-SD LoRA (Ren, Xia, Zhang et al.)
CompVis / Stability AI — Stable Diffusion 1.5 base

variants.summary: Generate photoreal portraits — sharper faces and skin texture than the other Hyper variants. More NSFW-permissive base.

entries.name: JuggernautXL Lightning (4-step SDXL)
entries.summary: High-resolution (1024×1024) text-to-image — SDXL quality in 4 sampling steps. Photoreal and fantasy from a single fine-tune. Plan for GPU + ~8 GB VRAM.
entries.description: SDXL fine-tune + Lightning distillation. 1024×1024, 4 steps, photorealistic + fantasy.
attributions: RunDiffusion — JuggernautXL Lightning (SDXL fine-tune + Lightning distillation)
Stability AI — Stable Diffusion XL base
ByteDance — SDXL Lightning method (Lin, Wang, Yang)

variants.summary: High-resolution (1024×1024) text-to-image — SDXL quality in 4 sampling steps. Photoreal and fantasy from a single fine-tune. Plan for GPU + ~8 GB VRAM.

entries.name: SD Turbo (1-4 step)
entries.summary: Stability AI's distilled SD 2.1 — 512x512 text-to-image in just 1-4 steps. The reference small-and-fast diffusion model: faster than the Hyper variants, broadly capable, no fine-tune bias.
entries.description: Stability AI's Adversarial Diffusion Distillation (ADD) of Stable Diffusion 2.1. Single CLIP-H text encoder (1024-dim), 512×512 latent decode, designed for 1-step inference with 4 steps as the quality sweet spot. Pick over the SD 1.5 Hyper variants when you want the canonical Stability baseline rather than a fine-tune; pick over SDXL Turbo when 512×512 is enough and you want roughly half the VRAM footprint. Subject to the Stability AI Community License — free for individuals and commercial use below $1M annual revenue.
attributions: Stability AI — SD Turbo (Sauer, Lorenz, Blattmann, Rombach, 2023). ADD distillation of Stable Diffusion 2.1.

variants.summary: Stability AI's distilled SD 2.1 — 512x512 text-to-image in just 1-4 steps. The reference small-and-fast diffusion model: faster than the Hyper variants, broadly capable, no fine-tune bias.

entries.name: SDXL Turbo (1-4 step)
entries.summary: Stability AI's distilled SDXL — 512×512 text-to-image in just 1-4 steps with SDXL's stronger prompt adherence and aesthetic baseline. Heavier than SD Turbo; lighter than full SDXL.
entries.description: Stability AI's Adversarial Diffusion Distillation (ADD) of SDXL base. Dual text encoders (CLIP-L 768 + OpenCLIP-G 1280, combined to 2048), 512×512 latent decode (not the 1024 of full SDXL), 1-4 step distillation. Pick over SD Turbo when prompt adherence + compositional quality matter more than throughput; pick over JuggernautXL Lightning when you want the Stability baseline rather than a community fine-tune, and 512×512 is enough output size. Subject to the Stability AI Community License — same $1M-revenue commercial threshold as SD Turbo.
attributions: Stability AI — SDXL Turbo (Sauer, Lorenz, Blattmann, Rombach, 2023). ADD distillation of Stable Diffusion XL.

variants.summary: Stability AI's distilled SDXL — 512×512 text-to-image in just 1-4 steps with SDXL's stronger prompt adherence and aesthetic baseline. Heavier than SD Turbo; lighter than full SDXL.

entries.name: Qwen2.5-Coder (GGUF)
entries.summary: Alibaba's coding-focused LLM family. Three sizes (1.5B / 3B / 7B) at GGUF quantizations for llama.cpp dispatch.
entries.description: All variants are llama.cpp-dispatched GGUFs with the same prompt template + chat schema. Pick by available RAM / latency budget.
attributions: Alibaba — Qwen 2.5 Coder
bartowski (GGUF quantization)

variants.summary: Small (1.5B-parameter) Apache-2.0 code-focused chat LLM from Alibaba. Fast to load and dispatch — the right pilot for validating SQL-defined LLM infrastructure.
Middle (3B-parameter) Apache-2.0 code-focused chat LLM from Alibaba. Meaningfully more coherent on multi-section HTML and short scripts than the 1.5B sibling.
Large (7B-parameter) Apache-2.0 code-focused chat LLM from Alibaba. Handles full single-file pages with embedded CSS/JS and multi-paragraph rationale.

entries.name: TinyLlama 1.1B Chat v1.0 (GGUF Q4_K_M)
entries.summary: Small (1.1B-parameter) Apache-2.0 chat LLM with a distinctly 2023-era voice. Useful for tonal A/B comparison against modern instruction-tuned models, and the cheapest LLM to dispatch in the zoo.
entries.description: Community-trained 1.1B chat model on the Zephyr template. Tiny (~700 MB) and fast — pick when latency or footprint trump quality. Registered as a pair of SQL-defined models: tinyllama_1_1b_chat (ChatCompleter, owns the GGUF session) and tinyllama_1_1b (TextGenerator, delegates to the chat model — zero additional VRAM cost).
attributions: TinyLlama community
TheBloke (GGUF quantization)

variants.summary: Small (1.1B-parameter) Apache-2.0 chat LLM with a distinctly 2023-era voice. Useful for tonal A/B comparison against modern instruction-tuned models, and the cheapest LLM to dispatch in the zoo.

entries.name: IBM Granite 3.1 1B A400M Instruct (GGUF Q4_K_M)
entries.summary: IBM's small (1B total, 400M active) mixture-of-experts chat LLM. Apache-2.0. Distinct 'enterprise-y' instruction-tuning voice — frequently bullet-pointed, careful with caveats.
entries.description: IBM's MoE-architecture small chat model. 1B total parameters, 400M active per token. Apache-2.0 fully unencumbered for commercial use. Registered as a pair of SQL-defined models: granite31_1b_chat (ChatCompleter, owns the GGUF session) and granite31_1b (TextGenerator, delegates to the chat model — zero additional VRAM cost).
attributions: IBM — Granite 3.1
bartowski (GGUF quantization)

variants.summary: IBM's small (1B total, 400M active) mixture-of-experts chat LLM. Apache-2.0. Distinct 'enterprise-y' instruction-tuning voice — frequently bullet-pointed, careful with caveats.

entries.name: Falcon3 1B Instruct (GGUF Q4_K_M)
entries.summary: TII (UAE) trained 1B chat LLM. Custom Falcon LLM License 2.0 (broadly permissive with acceptable-use policy). Distinct vocabulary and style — useful for family-comparison tonal A/B.
entries.description: Technology Innovation Institute's 1B-param chat-tuned model. ChatML chat template. Registered as a pair of SQL-defined models: falcon3_1b_chat (ChatCompleter, owns the GGUF session) and falcon3_1b (TextGenerator, delegates to the chat model — zero additional VRAM cost).
attributions: Technology Innovation Institute (TII) — Falcon3

variants.summary: TII (UAE) trained 1B chat LLM. Custom Falcon LLM License 2.0 (broadly permissive with acceptable-use policy). Distinct vocabulary and style — useful for family-comparison tonal A/B.

entries.name: Mistral 7B Instruct v0.3 (GGUF Q5_K_M)
entries.summary: Solid 7B-parameter Apache-2.0 chat model. The 'just give me a good general-purpose local LLM' default. Note: no native system role — prepend system text to the first user message if needed.
entries.description: Apache-2.0 7B instruction-tuned LLM. Solid general-purpose default. Registered as a pair of SQL-defined models: mistral_7b_chat (ChatCompleter, owns the GGUF session) and mistral_7b (TextGenerator, delegates to the chat model — zero additional VRAM cost).
attributions: Mistral AI
bartowski (GGUF quantization)

variants.summary: Solid 7B-parameter Apache-2.0 chat model. The 'just give me a good general-purpose local LLM' default. Note: no native system role — prepend system text to the first user message if needed.

entries.name: Llama 3.1 8B Instruct (GGUF Q4_K_M)
entries.summary: Meta's 8B chat model. The zoo's most reliable chat discipline. Requires accepting Meta's Llama 3.1 Community License before download.
entries.description: Meta's 8B-param instruction-tuned LLM. The most reliable chat discipline in the zoo — consistently emits `<|eot_id|>` on trivial prompts. Registered as a pair of SQL-defined models: llama31_8b_chat (ChatCompleter, owns the GGUF session) and llama31_8b (TextGenerator, delegates to the chat model — zero additional VRAM cost). Requires accepting the Llama 3.1 Community License before download.
attributions: © Meta Platforms, Inc. — Llama 3.1
bartowski (GGUF quantization)

variants.summary: Meta's 8B chat model. The zoo's most reliable chat discipline. Requires accepting Meta's Llama 3.1 Community License before download.

entries.name: Silero VAD (Voice Activity Detection)
entries.summary: Per-32 ms-frame speech-probability at 16 kHz. Tiny model; pick fp16 for half-disk deployment.
entries.description: All variants share the same VAD output and threshold semantics; only precision changes.
attributions: Silero Team — silero.ai (Silero VAD)

variants.summary: Emits a per-frame speech probability (one value every 32 ms at 16 kHz). Tiny (~2 MB). Pre-filter audio before transcribing it so the speech recognizer doesn't waste cycles on silence.
Same voice-activity detector as Silero VAD, half the disk. Pick when bundle size matters (containers, serverless cold-start, edge).

entries.name: Whisper
entries.summary: OpenAI's reference English+multilingual ASR. Pick a size: tiny/base for edge, small for default desktop, large-v3-turbo for top accuracy on GPU.
entries.description: All variants share the encoder-decoder architecture, tokenizer, and prompt format — they differ only in parameter count.
attributions: OpenAI — Whisper (Radford, Kim, Xu et al.)
onnx-community (ONNX export)

variants.summary: Smallest Whisper — fastest transcription, least accurate. Use on phones, embedded devices, or for ballpark transcription.
Convert speech to text in 99 languages. Balanced speed / accuracy default — start here for transcribing audio recordings.
Whisper at the next size up — better transcription accuracy, ~3× the cost of Base. Pick when quality matters more than throughput.
Whisper at the next size up — better transcription accuracy, ~3× the cost of Base. Pick when quality matters more than throughput.

entries.name: CLIP ViT-B/32
entries.summary: Embed images and text into the **same vector space** so you can search for images with words (or words with images). The original cross-modal model — the building block of zero-shot classification and CLIP-based search.
entries.description: OpenAI CLIP for image-text contrastive embeddings. Use for zero-shot image classification, cross-modal search.
attributions: OpenAI — CLIP (Radford, Kim, Hallacy et al.)
Joshua Lochner / Xenova (ONNX export)

variants.summary: Embed images and text into the **same vector space** so you can search for images with words (or words with images). The original cross-modal model — the building block of zero-shot classification and CLIP-based search.

entries.name: BiomedCLIP
entries.summary: CLIP for biomedical figures — embed radiology, histology, microscopy, and PMC paper figures into the same vector space as their captions. The biomedical counterpart to OpenAI CLIP.
entries.description: Microsoft BiomedCLIP. ViT-B/16 vision tower + PubMedBERT text tower projected into a shared 512-d space. Use for biomedical zero-shot classification, caption retrieval, and cross-modal search. Pick fp32 for the reference build; fp16 for half-disk GPU deployments.
attributions: Microsoft Research — BiomedCLIP (Zhang, Xu, Usuyama et al., 2023)
Heliosoph (open_clip + torch.onnx export from the upstream open_clip checkpoint)

variants.summary: Embed biomedical images and captions into a shared 512-d space. Pair with cosine_similarity / dot_product for zero-shot diagnosis labels, figure retrieval, and cross-modal search.
Same biomedical image-text embedder as BiomedCLIP fp32, half the disk. Wire boundary stays Float32; only internal weights and activations run in half precision.
